goal: Rebuild foundational strength, address cycling imbalances, support back health
equipment: Gym — machines, cables, dumbbells, stability ball, foam roller, bands
coach_note: This program is designed for a gradual return to strength training after time off. As a former Ironman athlete with a cycling background, you likely have excellent aerobic capacity but may have muscle imbalances typical of endurance cyclists: tight hip flexors, weak glutes, underdeveloped posterior chain, and rounded thoracic spine. We're prioritizing core stability, hip mobility, and posterior chain strength to support your back. Start light — the goal for weeks 1-4 is movement quality and building the habit. Intensity increases in Phase 2.

program_notes.phase_1_weeks_1_4: Foundation Phase — Focus on movement quality, mobility, and building consistency. Keep weights light (RPE 5-6). Master form before adding load.
program_notes.phase_2_weeks_5_8: Build Phase — Gradually increase weights (RPE 6-7). Add sets where indicated. Introduce more challenging progressions.
program_notes.back_care: Avoid heavy spinal loading initially. All core work emphasizes stability (anti-movement). If any exercise aggravates your back, stop and notify your coach.
program_notes.cycling_integration: Continue riding 2-3x per week at easy/moderate intensity. This program complements your cycling without overtraining.
program_notes.recovery: Sleep 7-8 hours. Consider magnesium supplementation. Foam roll and stretch daily, especially hip flexors and thoracic spine.

schedule.warmup: 10 min bike (easy) + Cat-Cow (10 reps), Hip Circles (10 each), Glute Bridges (15), Bird Dogs (10 each), 90/90 Hip Stretch (60s each)

exercises.name: Glute Bridge (floor)
exercises.notes: Squeeze glutes 2 sec at top, don't hyperextend back

exercises.name: Cable Pull-Through
exercises.notes: Hip hinge pattern — load glutes/hamstrings, not low back

exercises.name: Goblet Squat (light)
exercises.notes: Depth to comfort, keep chest tall, weight in heels

exercises.name: Leg Press (moderate)
exercises.notes: Feet high on platform to reduce knee stress, control descent

exercises.name: Seated Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Slow 3-sec eccentric, feel hamstrings work

exercises.name: Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Full ROM, pause at top

exercises.name: Side-Lying Clamshell
exercises.notes: Hip external rotation — keep pelvis stable

schedule.cooldown: Hip flexor stretch (90s each), Pigeon pose (60s each), Foam roll quads and glutes (2 min each)

schedule.warmup: 5 min row machine + Arm Circles (20 each), Band Pull-Aparts (15), Wall Angels (10), Thoracic Rotations (8 each)

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Press
exercises.notes: 30° incline, shoulder-friendly angle, control descent

exercises.name: Seated Cable Row (wide)
exercises.notes: Squeeze shoulder blades, open chest — counteracts cycling posture

exercises.name: Dumbbell Shoulder Press (seated)
exercises.notes: Neutral grip if shoulders feel better, don't arch back

exercises.name: Face Pulls
exercises.notes: High pull to face, external rotate at end — posture corrector

exercises.name: Cable Chest Fly
exercises.notes: Light weight, feel the stretch, build chest mobility

exercises.name: Tricep Pushdown
exercises.notes: Keep elbows pinned, full extension

exercises.name: Dead Hang
exercises.notes: Decompress spine, stretch lats and shoulders

schedule.cooldown: Doorway pec stretch (30s each), Child's pose (60s), Foam roll thoracic spine (2 min)

schedule.warmup: 10 min easy walk/bike + Full body stretch flow (5 min)

exercises.name: Dead Bug
exercises.notes: Press low back into floor, move slowly — core stability king

exercises.name: Bird Dog
exercises.notes: Anti-rotation, extend opposite arm/leg, keep hips level

exercises.name: Pallof Press
exercises.notes: Anti-rotation — resist cable pull, brace core

exercises.name: Side Plank (modified)
exercises.notes: From knees if needed, stack hips, don't let hips sag

exercises.name: Glute Bridge March
exercises.notes: Hold bridge, lift one foot at a time — pelvic stability

exercises.name: Cat-Cow
exercises.notes: Slow and controlled, feel each vertebra move

exercises.name: World's Greatest Stretch
exercises.notes: Hip flexor + thoracic rotation — essential for cyclists

exercises.name: 90/90 Hip Transitions
exercises.notes: Improve hip internal/external rotation

exercises.name: Foam Roll Sequence
exercises.notes: IT band, quads, glutes, thoracic spine, lats

schedule.cooldown: Supine spinal twist (60s each), Happy baby pose (60s), Deep breathing (2 min)

schedule.warmup: 10 min bike (easy) + Leg Swings (10 each), Bodyweight Squats (10), Single-Leg Balance (30s each), Ankle Circles (10 each)

exercises.name: Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Quad isolation — avoid if knees hurt, use light weight

exercises.name: Goblet Squat
exercises.notes: Sit between legs, chest up, controlled tempo

exercises.name: Step-Up (low box)
exercises.notes: 8-12 inch box, drive through front heel, don't push off back leg

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift (DB)
exercises.notes: Hinge at hips, soft knee bend, feel hamstrings stretch

exercises.name: Single-Leg Glute Bridge
exercises.notes: Pelvis stays level, drive through heel

exercises.name: Seated Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Soleus focus — important for cycling efficiency

exercises.name: Adductor Machine
exercises.notes: Inner thigh strength — often weak in cyclists

schedule.cooldown: Standing quad stretch (30s each), Seated forward fold (60s), Figure-4 stretch (60s each)

schedule.warmup: 5 min row machine + Scapular Retractions (15), Band Pull-Aparts (15), Prone Y-T-W (8 each), Shoulder CARS (5 each)

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown (neutral grip)
exercises.notes: Initiate with lats, not biceps — lean back slightly

exercises.name: Seated Cable Row (close grip)
exercises.notes: Pull to belly button, squeeze shoulder blades 2 sec

exercises.name: Reverse Fly (machine or DB)
exercises.notes: Rear delts and mid-traps — posture muscles

exercises.name: Face Pulls
exercises.notes: Pull high, external rotate — can't do too many of these

exercises.name: Bicep Curl (DB)
exercises.notes: Controlled, no swinging — supinate at top

exercises.name: Prone Y Raise
exercises.notes: Face down on incline bench, arms form Y — lower trap activation

exercises.name: Dead Hang
exercises.notes: Spinal decompression — relax and breathe

schedule.cooldown: Lat stretch (30s each), Cross-body shoulder stretch (30s each), Chest opener on foam roller (2 min)
